game_info.title: Untitled Game
game_info.concept: Players drag and drop various geometric blocks onto a 9x9 grid, aiming to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points, with the game ending when no more blocks can be placed from the current set of three available. Blocks cannot be rotated, and strategic placement is key to maximizing space and achieving high scores, with combo bonuses awarded for clearing multiple lines simultaneously or consecutively.
game_info.description: Block Blast is a puzzle game where you place various geometric blocks on a 9x9 grid. Your goal is to fill entire rows or columns to clear them and earn points. You have three blocks available at a time, and must strategically place them to maximize your score. The game ends when you can't place any of the available blocks. Earn bonus points by clearing multiple lines at once or in sequence!
game_info.controls:
Arrow Left/Right: Move the selected block horizontally
Arrow Up/Down: Move the selected block vertically
Z: Cycle through the available blocks
Space: Place the currently selected block on the grid
Esc: Pause the game
R: Restart the game
Enter: Start the game
game_info.automated_testing:
<TEST_1>
<test_description>Tests basic game functionality by randomly placing blocks on the grid to ensure the game compiles without errors and responds properly to inputs.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Cycles through available blocks and places them in valid positions on the grid using sticky keys for movement and placement.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game runs without errors, blocks are placed on the grid, and the game eventually reaches a game over state when no more valid moves are available.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_1>
<TEST_2>
<test_description>Tests winning gameplay by strategically placing blocks to clear lines and achieve a high score.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Prioritizes placing blocks to complete rows and columns, focusing on clearing multiple lines simultaneously for bonus points.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The test successfully clears multiple lines, earns a high score, and demonstrates that the game can be won through strategic block placement.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_2>
<TEST_3>
<test_description>Tests the line clearing mechanics by specifically targeting row and column completion.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Places blocks to intentionally complete rows and columns, verifying that lines are properly cleared and points are awarded.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>Lines are correctly cleared when completed, the grid updates accordingly, and appropriate points are awarded including any combo bonuses.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_3>
<TEST_4>
<test_description>Tests the block selection and cycling mechanism to ensure all blocks can be accessed and placed.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Repeatedly cycles through available blocks and places them, verifying that new blocks are generated and selection works properly.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>All blocks can be selected, cycling works correctly, and new blocks are generated when others are placed on the grid.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_4>
<TEST_5>
<test_description>Tests game over detection by filling the grid in a way that leaves no valid placement options.</test_description>
<strategy_description>Strategically places blocks to create a nearly-full grid with specific gaps that don't match any of the available blocks.</strategy_description>
<expected_outcome>The game correctly detects when no more valid placements are possible and transitions to the game over state.</expected_outcome>
</TEST_5>
